DOCUMENT.source-infobase-title: The Rules of the City of New York

RECORD.PARA: Whenever a respondent is found in violation of any of the following provisions of the New York City Administrative Code or Rules of the City of New York, any civil penalties imposed by a Hearing Officer pursuant to 48 RCNY § 6-17(a) and/or any default penalties imposed pursuant to 48 RCNY § 6-20(b) in accordance with Section 1049-a(d)(1)(d) of the Charter and/or any civil penalties imposed for admissions of violation(s) pursuant to 48 RCNY § 6-09(a) or late admissions pursuant to 48 RCNY § 3-17 will be imposed pursuant to the penalty schedules set forth below. [ALP S-014]
(Added City Record 2/22/2017, eff. 3/24/2017; amended City Record 5/8/2017, eff. 6/7/2017) [ALP S-014]
All Citations are to the NYC Administrative Code unless otherwise indicated. [ALP S-010]

An admission or stipulation penalty is imposed if there is a "Y" (Yes) in the Stipulation or Admission column in the Penalty Schedule rather than a "N" (No), and if a stipulation is offered and accepted, or the violation is admitted, at a hearing. [ALP S-105]

Definition of 2nd and/or 3rd and/or 4th offense: By the same respondent of the same provision of law, order, rule or regulation as the previous violation and, if the respondent is the owner, agent, lessee or other person in control of the premises with respect to which the violation occurred, at the same premises as the previous violation (all violations committed within two years). [ALP S-010]

Pursuant to 48 RCNY § 3-17, a late admit fee of $30.00 will be added to all the below listed penalties for a failure to submit a payment by mail within 30 days of the mailing date of the default order issued against respondent. [ALP S-014]

Asterisk (*): In connection with § 24-231(a), a zero penalty shall be imposed for admission of a first offense upon submission to DEP (within 30 days of NOV issuance unless extended by DEP) of acceptable certification of compliance as set forth in § 24-231(b)(1). [ALP S-079]

PARA.CELLFORMAT: Noise from sound device exceeding permit levels
Reduce noise to conform to permit forthwith
Failed to Comply with Commissioner's Order to Provide Access for Testing
Refused DEP entry into public area(s) of premises
Refusal to allow authorized employee to perform sound testing
Operating equipment without a valid registration
Cease interference with adm. personnel - forthwith
False/misleading statements: unlawful repro/alteration of documents
Failure to post certificate or tunneling permit
Failure to comply with noise abatement contract requirements
Causing or permitting unreasonable noise (7 a.m. to 10 p.m.)
Causing or permitting unreasonable noise (10 p.m. to 7 a.m.)
Causing or permitting unreasonable noise for commercial activities or purposes or through a device installed within or upon a building
Failure to comply with Commissioner's Order or mitigation measures re noise from refuse collection facility
Use of mobile telephones in a place of public performance
Failure to adopt/implement Noise Mitigation Plan for construction site
Inadequate / insufficiently detailed noise mitigation plan
Provide additional information to Noise Plan forthwith
Failure to ensure that all construction workers are familiar with noise mitigation plan
Inform workers about noise mitigation plan requirements forthwith
Failure to keep/have available for inspection copy of noise mitigation plan
Failure to amend noise mitigation plan
Failure to file noise mitigation plan when required
Cease construction activities at impermissible times/days forthwith
Failure to submit certification for emergency work within 3 days of starting work
Excessive noise as measured from a residential receiving property above 8dB(A).
Stop operation of device causing exceedance.
Noise from a construction site in excess of 85 dB(A) that is not within 200 feet of a residential receptor.
Stop operation of device causing exceedance.
Noise from a construction site in excess of 80 dB(A) within 200 feet of a residential receptor.
Stop operation of device causing exceedance.
Noise from street construction work in excess of 85 dB(A).
Stop operation of device causing exceedance.
Stop work and/or operation of device that is the cause of the stop work order.
Construction work not in compliance with noise mitigation plan
Sell / offer / operate / permit operation of refuse compacting vehicle producing excessive noise
Stop operation of compacting vehicle forthwith
Operate / cause operation of refuse compacting vehicle producing excessive noise (11 p.m. to 7 a.m.)
Stop operation of compacting vehicle forthwith
Operating air compressor without appropriate muffler w/no exhaust leaks
Stop operation of air compressor forthwith
Excessive noise from air compressor (measured @ 1 meter)
Stop operation of air compressor forthwith
Excessive noise from air compressor (measured @ receiving property)
Stop operation of air compressor forthwith
Noise from circulation device in excess of 42 dB(A)
Stop operation of circulation device forthwith
Cumulative impact from circulation device exceeded 45 dB(A)
Stop operation of circulation device forthwith
Failure to reduce cumulative impact from multiple circulation devices exceeding 50 dB(A)
Reduce cumulative impact noise from circulation device forthwith
Stop operation of construction device forthwith
Stop operation of engine exhaust forthwith
Unreasonable noise from handling/transporting of container or construction material
Stop handling and transportation of construction material forthwith
Operation/caused operation of paving breaker w/o pneumatic discharge muffler
Use paving breaker with pneumatic discharge muffler forthwith
Sold/offer for sale/operate/permit operation of paving breaker producing over 95 dB(A)
Stop operation of paving breaker forthwith
Made / caused / permitted music from commercial establishment in excess of permitted levels
Cease operation of commercial music forthwith
Violation of variance from limits set forth in 24-231(a)
Cease operation of commercial music forthwith
Excessive noise from sound source @ commercial or business establishment
Stop operation of sound source forthwith
Stop operation of personal audio device forthwith
Unreasonable noise – personal audio device (public right-of-way)
Stop operation of personal audio device forthwith
Unreasonable noise – personal audio device (motor vehicle)
Stop operation of personal audio device forthwith
Excess noise from sound reproduction device on rapid transit (subway, bus, ferry)
Stop operation of sound reproduction device forthwith
Permitting animal to cause unreasonable noise
Cease permitting animal to cause unreasonable noise forthwith
Excess noise from motor vehicles (10,000 lbs. or less)
Stop operating vehicle that causes excessive noise forthwith
Stop operating motorcycle that causes excessive noise forthwith
Excess noise from motor vehicles (10,000 lbs. or greater)
Stop operating vehicle that causes excessive noise forthwith
Non-emergency use of compression brake system
Stop using compression brake for non-emergencies forthwith

Causing or permitting the total sound from motor vehicle operating on a public right-of-way to exceed the sound level set forth in section 386 of the New York State Vehicle and Traffic Law and any applicable rules
Stop causing or permitting the total sound of such a motor vehicle to exceed the sound level set forth in section 386 of the New York State Vehicle and Traffic Law and any applicable rules forthwith
Unauthorized use of motor vehicle claxon
Unauthorized use of motor vehicle air horn/gong
Cease use of air horn forthwith
Improper use of sound signal device (food vendor)
Cease use of sound signal device forthwith
Improper audible burglar alarm/no automatic termination
Cease use of burglar alarm forthwith
Audible status indicator on motor vehicle in operation
Cease use of status indicator forthwith
Vehicle owner failure to display owner's local precinct number
Unauthorized use of emergency signal device
Failed to file certification regarding test of emergency signal device
Operating lawn care devices at unauthorized times or so as to create unreasonable noise
Stop operation of lawn care device forthwith
Operation of leaf blower without muffler
Stop operation of lawn care device forthwith
Unreasonable noise from sound reproduction device
Cease operation of sound reproduction device forthwith
Unreasonable noise from sound reproduction device for commercial/bus. advert. Purposes
Cease operation of sound reproduction device forthwith
Failure to have operating certificate or tunneling permit
Miscellaneous violation of Noise Control Code orRules
Failed To Conspicuously Post an Accurate and Complete Construction Noise Mitigation Plan (CNMP)
Failed To Self-Certify Equipment Maintenance in CNMP
Failed to Exercise Noise Mitigation Option Within 5 Business Days of Construction Tool Exceeding Noise Standard
Replace tool with equipment that complies with noise standard forthwith
Failed to Equip Construction Equipment with Noise Reduction Device
Equip construction equipment with noise reduction device
Failed to Mitigate Noise From Internal Combustion Engine
Mitigate noise from internal combustion engine forthwith
Failed to Cover Compressors / Generators / Pumps with Noise-Insulating Fabric
Cover compressor with noise insulating fabric forthwith
Failed to Use Perimeter Noise Barriers
Failed to Create and Utilize Noise Mitigation Training Program
Create and utilize training program forthwith
Failed to Coordinate Work Schedule with Sensitive Facility Receptor
Failure to Comply with CNMP or File Alternative Plan w/in 3 Days of Inspection
Comply with CNMP or file Alternative Plan forthwith
Failed to Utilize Noise Barrier on Sandblasting Perimeter Barrier
Failed to Use Specified Pile Driver When Working w/in 100 ft. of Receptor
Failed to Equip Pile Driver w/Exhaust Muffler
Equip pile driver with muffler forthwith
Failed to Pre-Augur / Pre-Trench Pile Holes
Failed to Properly Secure Impact Cushions to Piles
Failed to Properly Construct Portable Noise Barrier
Failed to Equip Jackhammer With Effective Muffler
Failed to Properly Construct Portable Noise Barrier
Exceeded Maximum Height of 15 Feet for Free-Standing Barrier
Reduce barrier height to 15 Feet forthwith
Failed to Use Multiple Tents for Multiple Jackhammers
Failed to Move Noise Tents as Jackhammer Work Progresses
Failed to Use Double Layer of Mitigation During Emergency Jackhammering w/in 500 ft. of Residential Receptor
Use double layer of mitigation forthwith
Failed to Use Tents on Either Side of Jackhammer Where Surrounded by Receptors
Failed to Wrap Noise Shroud Around Head of Hoe Ram w/in 200 ft. of Receptor
Failed to Properly Construct Portable Noise Barrier
Failed to Lay Heavy Rubber Blast Mats Over Blast Site
Failed to Properly Construct Portable Noise Barrier
Failed to Cover Vac-Truck's Suction Component w/Noise-Reducing Enclosure
Cover vac truck with noise reducing enclosure forthwith
Failed to Properly Construct Portable Noise Barrier
Failed to Equip Truck with Effective Muffler
Equip truck with effective muffler forthwith
Failed to Keep Housing Doors Closed During Engine Operation
Close housing doors during engine operation forthwith
Failed to Properly Construct Portable Noise Barrier
Failed to Equip Crane with Effective Muffler
Equip crane with effective muffler forthwith
Failed to Equip Auger Drill Rig with Effective Muffler
Equip auger drill with effective muffler forthwith
Failed to Lubricate All Moving Parts of Auger Drill Rig
Lubricate all moving parts of auger drill forthwith
Failed to Properly Clear Debris From Drill Bits
Clean all debris from drill bits forthwith
Failed to Properly Construct Portable Noise Barriers
Failed to Properly Install Street Plates
Failed to Equip Work Vehicles with Proper Backup Alarms
Equip work vehicles with proper backup alarm forthwith
Failed to Properly Construct Portable Noise Barriers
Failed to Properly Construct Portable Noise Barriers
Failed to Use Multiple Tents During Use of Multiple Concrete Saws
Failed to Move Noise Tent As Concrete Saw Work Progressed
Failed to Use Double Layer of Mitigation for Noise Barrier During Emergency Concrete Sawing within 500 ft. of Residential Receptor
Use double layer of mitigation forthwith
Failed to Use Two Tents on Either Side of Saw When Surrounded by Receptors
Use two tents on either side of saw forthwith
Failed to File Alternative Noise Mitigation Plan (ANMP)
Failed to Conspicuously Post Utility Noise Mitigation Plan (UNMP)
Failed to Self-Certify Equipment Maintenance in UNMP
Failed to Exercise Noise Mitigation Option within 5 Days of Construction Tool Exceeding Noise Standard
Replace tool with equipment that complies with noise standard forthwith
Failed to Equip Construction Tool with Noise Reduction Device
Equip tool with noise reduction device
Failed to Comply with Additional Noise Mitigation Measures for Specialized Vehicles
Use additional noise mitigation measures for specialized trucks forthwith
Failed to Cover Equipment with Noise-Insulating Fabric
Cover equipment with noise insulating fabric forthwith
Failed to Properly Install and Secure Street Plates
Failed to Notify Residents within 200 Feet of Construction Activity
Failed to Respond to Noise Complaints/Notice from DEP
Failed to Create and Utilize Noise Mitigation Training Program
Create and utilize training program forthwith
Failed to Coordinate Work Schedule with Sensitive Receptor Owner
Failed to Correct Excessive Noise Condition/File ANMP
Correct excessive noise and/or file ANMP forthwith

RECORD.PARA: (Added City Record 2/22/2017, eff. 3/24/2017; amended City Record 5/8/2017, eff. 6/7/2017; amended City Record 9/5/2018, eff. 10/5/2018; amended City Record 10/20/2021, eff. 11/19/2021; amended City Record 2/17/2022, eff. 3/19/2022; amended City Record 11/18/2022, eff. 11/18/2022; amended City Record 12/16/2024, eff. 1/15/2025; amended City Record 2/28/2025, eff. 3/30/2025) [ALP S-107]
